description_long: This is a small, single-engine general aviation airplane, likely a Cessna, parked on a paved surface. The fuselage is primarily white with blue and red accent stripes, and the registration number is visible on the tail. The wings are straight and mounted high on the fuselage, with a fixed tricycle landing gear configuration. The cockpit is enclosed and positioned at the front of the fuselage, featuring side windows and a windshield. The tail section has a conventional design with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The airplane appears to be covered with a protective tarp over the cockpit and engine area, suggesting it is not currently in use.
description: A small, white single-engine airplane with high-mounted wings, tricycle landing gear, and a covered cockpit, parked on pavement.
description_view_0: The airplane is seen from a rear-side angle, showing the tail, registration number, and the covered cockpit area.
description_view_1: A direct rear view highlights the full wingspan, tail assembly, and the protective cover over the cockpit and engine.
description_view_2: A front-side angle reveals the nose, propeller area under the cover, and the forward cockpit windows.
description_view_3: A direct front view displays the nose, covered propeller, and the symmetry of the wings and landing gear.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing, straight configuration with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single piston engine in the nose, covered by a tarp.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed tricycle landing gear with two main wheels and a single nose wheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with side windows and a windshield, likely single or dual seat.
